content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 4, 1), (4, 6, 8), (4, 9, 17), (3, 9, 20), (0, 5, 21), (6, 8, 21), (0, 8, 22), (1, 9, 22), (3, 5, 22), (1, 3, 24), (5, 8, 24), (4, 7, 26), (1, 8, 27), (4, 5, 27), (7, 9, 27), (0, 3, 28), (2, 3, 29)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 6 exactly at time 8.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

For time 8:

* Edge (3, 4, 1) has node 3 and time 1. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (4, 6, 8) has node 4 and time 8. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (4, 9, 17) has node 4 and time 17. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 9, 20) has node 3 and time 20. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 5, 21) has node 0 and time 21. -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 8, 21) has node 6 and time 21. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 8, 22) has node 0 and time 22. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 9, 22) has node 1 and time 22. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 5, 22) has node 3 and time 22. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 3, 24) has node 1 and time 24. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 8, 24) has node 5 and time 24. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 7, 26) has node 4 and time 26. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 8, 27) has node 1 and time 27. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 5, 27) has node 4 and time 27. -> Ignore.
* Edge (7, 9, 27) has node 7 and time 27. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 3, 28) has node 0 and time 28. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 3, 29) has node 2 and time 29. -> Ignore.

The nodes matched are 4 and 6.

Answer: [4, 6]
